title: Process for covering a wire with a layer of polymer
abstract: The invention relates to a process for coating the surface of a moving wire (50) with a thin solid polymer layer. The process includes the step of providing a deposition device (100) having a compartment (108d') of a chamber (108) that contains two rotatable rings (110) and two wheels including an upper wheel (114) and a lower wheel (114') having circumferential grooves (114b, 114b') in which the two rings (110) are lodged. The wheels (114, 114') are placed vertically on either side of the wire (50) by means of the grooves (114c, 114c') such that a predetermined contact force between the wheels (114, 114') and the wire (50) makes it possible, by friction, when the wire (50) moves at constant speed, to rotate the wheels (114, 114') and the rings (110) too. The device also includes a tank (106) containing a liquid polymer solution (75). The wire (50) is conveyed at a predetermined constant speed, and the rotatable rings (110) are rotated, these conveying an amount of the liquid polymer solution (75). The process also includes the step of evaporating the solvent from the liquid polymer solution (75) deposited at the surface of the wire (50).
claims:
Claims 1. A process for coating the surface of a moving wire (50) with a thin solid polymer layer, wherein the process comprises the following steps: providing a deposition device (100) having a compartment (108d') of a chamber (108) that contains two rotatable rings (110) and two wheels including an upper wheel (114) and a lower wheel (114') having circumferential grooves (114b, 114b') in which the two rings (110) are lodged, and in which compartment the wheels (114, 114') are placed vertically on either side of the wire (50) by means of the grooves (314c, 314c') such that a predetermined contact force between the wheels (114, 114') and the wire (50) makes it possible, by friction, when the wire (50) moves at constant speed, to rotate the wheels (114, 114') and, by means of the lower wheel (114'), the rings (110) too, and a tank (106) containing a liquid polymer solution (75); conveying the wire (50) at a predetermined constant speed; rotating the rotatable rings (110), these rings conveying an amount of the liquid polymer solution (75), and these rings bringing it to a point of tangential contact between the grooves (114c, 114c') of the wheels (114, 114') and the wire (50), which at the same time passes through an orifice (126) formed by the grooves (114c, 114c') and located between the grooves (114b, 114b'), in order to coat the surface of the wire (50) while it is moving; and evaporating the solvent from the liquid polymer solution (75) deposited at the surface of the wire (50). 2. A process according to claim 1, wherein the step of evaporating the solvent from the liquid polymer solution (75) deposited at the surface of the wire (50) comprises: a process for increasing the temperature of the wire (50) in a heating device (400) until an evaporation temperature of the solvent is attained; and a process for maintaining the temperature of the wire (50) in a temperature maintaining device (500) until the complete evaporation of the solvent is obtained. 3. A process according to claim 2, wherein the process for increasing 

 the temperature of the wire (50) is carried out by means of an induction coil in the heating device (400). 4. A process according to claim 2 or claim 3, additionally comprising the step of creating a partial vacuum simultaneously in the spaces surrounding the wire (50) of the deposition device (100), of the heating device (400) and of the temperature maintaining device (500). 5. A process according to any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein a surplus of the polymer solution (75) is conveyed to the tank (106). 6. A process according to claim 5, wherein the surplus of the polymer solution (75) is conveyed to the tank (106) by the rotation of the rings (110) and by the effect of gravity. 7. A process according to claim 5 or claim 6, additionally comprising a step of calibrating the thickness of the deposited layer of the polymer solution (75) by means of a calibration bushing (128) provided with an orifice at its centre and mounted in the compartment (108d') of the chamber (108) in order to make the surplus of the polymer solution (75) fall into the tank (106). 8. A process according to any one of claims 1 to 7, additionally comprising the dipping of a lower portion (110a) of each ring (110) in the liquid polymer solution (75) by means of an opening (108e) in the bottom of the compartment (108d') of the chamber (108) that corresponds with at least one portion of the tank (106). 9. A system (10) for producing a wire (50) coated with a layer of solid polymer using a chosen polymer dissolved in a liquid solvent, the system (10) comprising a series of devices (100, 400, 500) that together define a process according to any one of Claims 1 to 8. 10. A system (10) according to claim 9, wherein the series of devices (100, 400, 500) comprises the deposition device (100) that carries out a process for 

 depositing liquid polymer solution on the surface of the wire (50) while it is moving. 11. A system (10) according to claim 10, wherein the series of devices comprises: a heating device (400) that increases the temperature of the wire (50) to initiate the evaporation of the solvent from the layer deposited on the wire (50); and a temperature maintaining device (500) that maintains the temperature of the wire (50) until the complete evaporation of the solvent is obtained. 12. A system (10) according to claim 11, wherein the devices (100, 400, 500) are connected together in a leaktight manner with a free passage of the wire (50) from one to the other, and the tightness seals with respect to a partial vacuum are positioned at the inlet of the deposition device (100) and at the outlet of the temperature maintaining device (500). 13. A wire (50) coated according to a process according to any one of claims 1 to 8.
